yield.note: primary collection; additional 2-3 liters may be collected and reserved for subsequent distillation

ingredients.name_it: Fiori di arancio recenti, colti e mondati
ingredients.name_en: Fresh orange blossoms, picked and cleaned

steps_en: Heat the salted water in the still's cucurbit nearly to boiling.
Attach the head to the coil and seal with lute.
Begin distillation, collecting 8 liters using the Florentine receiver.
To prepare double or triple strength water, collect one-third or half the stated quantity, or redistill the obtained liquid over a fresh quantity of blossoms proportional to the desired strength.
notes_en: The method allows for adjustment of concentration by varying the proportion of blossoms to water or by redistillation.

ingredients.name_it: Foglie, sommità fiorite e steli freschi di assenzio
ingredients.name_en: Fresh leaves, flowering tops, and stems of absinthe

steps_en: Macerate the chopped or broken plant material for 24 hours.
The water will initially appear milky (lactescent); upon resting, the essential oil separates.
notes_en: The same method is used to prepare waters of marjoram, rue, oregano, and citronella.
The same procedure is used to prepare waters of hyssop, melilot, peppermint, curly mint, rosemary, sage, and thyme.
